name: thrift

metadata.name: thrift
metadata.summary: Python bindings for the Apache Thrift RPC system

documentation:
Thrift Python Software Library

License
=======

or more contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file
distributed with this work for additional information
regarding copyright ownership. The ASF licenses this file
to you under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the
"License"); you may not use this file except in compliance
with the License. You may obtain a copy of the License at

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing,
software distributed under the License is distributed on an

KIND, either express or implied. See the License for the
specific language governing permissions and limitations
under the License.

Using Thrift with Python
========================

Thrift is provided as a set of Python packages. The top level package is
thrift, and there are subpackages for the protocol, transport, and server
code. Each package contains modules using standard Thrift naming conventions
(i.e. TProtocol, TTransport) and implementations in corresponding modules
(i.e. TSocket).  There is also a subpackage reflection, which contains
the generated code for the reflection structures.

The Python libraries can be installed manually using the provided setup.py
file, or automatically using the install hook provided via autoconf/automake.
To use the latter, become superuser and do make install.

classes.name: server.THttpServer.ResponseException
documentation:
Allows handlers to override the HTTP response

Normally, THttpServer always sends a 200 response.  If a handler wants
to override this behavior (e.g., to simulate a misconfigured or
overloaded web server during testing), it can raise a ResponseException.
The function passed to the constructor will be called with the
RequestHandler as its only argument.  Note that this is irrelevant
for ONEWAY requests, as the HTTP response must be sent before the
RPC is processed.

classes.name: server.THttpServer.THttpServer

methods.name: server.THttpServer.THttpServer.__init__
documentation:
Set up protocol factories and HTTP (or HTTPS) server.

See BaseHTTPServer for server_address.
See TServer for protocol factories.

To make a secure server, provide the named arguments:
* cafile    - to validate clients [optional]
* cert_file - the server cert
* key_file  - the server's key

A simple HTTP-based Thrift server

This class is not very performant, but it is useful (for example) for
acting as a mock version of an Apache-based PHP Thrift endpoint.
Also important to note the HTTP implementation pretty much violates the
transport/protocol/processor/server layering, by performing the transport
functions here.  This means things like oneway handling are oddly exposed.

classes.name: protocol.THeaderProtocol.THeaderProtocol
documentation:
A framed protocol with headers and payload transforms.

THeaderProtocol frames other Thrift protocols and adds support for optional
out-of-band headers. The currently supported subprotocols are
TBinaryProtocol and TCompactProtocol. When used as a client, the
subprotocol to frame can be chosen with the `default_protocol` parameter to
the constructor.

It's also possible to apply transforms to the encoded message payload. The
only transform currently supported is to gzip.

When used in a server, THeaderProtocol can accept messages from
non-THeaderProtocol clients if allowed (see `allowed_client_types`). This
includes framed and unframed transports and both TBinaryProtocol and
TCompactProtocol. The server will respond in the appropriate dialect for
the connected client. HTTP clients are not currently supported.

THeaderProtocol does not currently support THTTPServer, TNonblockingServer,
or TProcessPoolServer.

See doc/specs/HeaderFormat.md for details of the wire format.

functions.name: transport.sslcompat.legacy_validate_callback
documentation:
legacy method to validate the peer's SSL certificate, and to check
the commonName of the certificate to ensure it matches the hostname we
used to make this connection.  Does not support subjectAltName records
in certificates.

raises TTransportException if the certificate fails validation.

methods.name: TMultiplexedProcessor.TMultiplexedProcessor.registerDefault
documentation:
If a non-multiplexed processor connects to the server and wants to
communicate, use the given processor to handle it.  This mechanism
allows servers to upgrade from non-multiplexed to multiplexed in a
backwards-compatible way and still handle old clients.

classes.name: protocol.TBinaryProtocol.TBinaryProtocol
documentation: Binary implementation of the Thrift protocol driver.

classes.name: protocol.TBinaryProtocol.TBinaryProtocolAccelerated
documentation:
C-Accelerated version of TBinaryProtocol.

This class does not override any of TBinaryProtocol's methods,
but the generated code recognizes it directly and will call into
our C module to do the encoding, bypassing this object entirely.
We inherit from TBinaryProtocol so that the normal TBinaryProtocol
encoding can happen if the fastbinary module doesn't work for some
reason.  (TODO(dreiss): Make this happen sanely in more cases.)
To disable this behavior, pass fallback=False constructor argument.

In order to take advantage of the C module, just use
TBinaryProtocolAccelerated instead of TBinaryProtocol.

NOTE:  This code was contributed by an external developer.
       The internal Thrift team has reviewed and tested it,
       but we cannot guarantee that it is production-ready.
       Please feel free to report bugs and/or success stories
       to the public mailing list.

classes.name: transport.THttpClient.THttpClient

methods.name: transport.THttpClient.THttpClient.__init__
documentation:
THttpClient supports two different types of construction:

THttpClient(host, port, path) - deprecated
THttpClient(uri, [port=<n>, path=<s>, cafile=<filename>, cert_file=<filename>, key_file=<filename>, ssl_context=<context>])

Only the second supports https.  To properly authenticate against the server,
provide the client's identity by specifying cert_file and key_file.  To properly
authenticate the server, specify either cafile or ssl_context with a CA defined.
NOTE: if both cafile and ssl_context are defined, ssl_context will override cafile.
Http implementation of TTransport base.

classes.name: server.TServer.TServer
documentation:
Base interface for a server, which must have a serve() method.

Three constructors for all servers:
1) (processor, serverTransport)
2) (processor, serverTransport, transportFactory, protocolFactory)
3) (processor, serverTransport,
    inputTransportFactory, outputTransportFactory,
    inputProtocolFactory, outputProtocolFactory)

classes.name: server.TServer.TSimpleServer
documentation: Simple single-threaded server that just pumps around one transport.

classes.name: server.TServer.TThreadedServer
documentation: Threaded server that spawns a new thread per each connection.

classes.name: server.TServer.TThreadPoolServer

methods.name: server.TServer.TThreadPoolServer.setNumThreads
documentation: Set the number of worker threads that should be created

methods.name: server.TServer.TThreadPoolServer.serveThread
documentation: Loop around getting clients from the shared queue and process them.

methods.name: server.TServer.TThreadPoolServer.serveClient
documentation: Process input/output from a client for as long as possible

methods.name: server.TServer.TThreadPoolServer.serve
documentation: Start a fixed number of worker threads and put client into a queue
Server with a fixed size pool of threads which service requests.

classes.name: server.TServer.TForkingServer
documentation:
A Thrift server that forks a new process for each request

This is more scalable than the threaded server as it does not cause
GIL contention.

Note that this has different semantics from the threading server.
Specifically, updates to shared variables will no longer be shared.
It will also not work on windows.

This code is heavily inspired by SocketServer.ForkingMixIn in the
Python stdlib.

modules.name: transport.TZlibTransport
documentation:
TZlibTransport provides a compressed transport and transport factory
class, using the python standard library zlib module to implement
data compression.

distributed with this work for additional information

classes.name: transport.TZlibTransport.TZlibTransportFactory

methods.name: transport.TZlibTransport.TZlibTransportFactory.getTransport
documentation:
Wrap a transport, trans, with the TZlibTransport
compressed transport class, returning a new
transport to the caller.

@param compresslevel: The zlib compression level, ranging
from 0 (no compression) to 9 (best compression).  Defaults to 9.
@type compresslevel: int

This method returns a TZlibTransport which wraps the
passed C{trans} TTransport derived instance.

Factory transport that builds zlib compressed transports.

This factory caches the last single client/transport that it was passed
and returns the same TZlibTransport object that was created.

This caching means the TServer class will get the _same_ transport
object for both input and output transports from this factory.
(For non-threaded scenarios only, since the cache only holds one object)

The purpose of this caching is to allocate only one TZlibTransport where
only one is really needed (since it must have separate read/write buffers),
and makes the statistics from getCompSavings() and getCompRatio()
easier to understand.

classes.name: transport.TZlibTransport.TZlibTransport

methods.name: transport.TZlibTransport.TZlibTransport.__init__
transport.TZlibTransport.TZlibTransport._reinit_buffers
documentation:
Internal method to initialize/reset the internal StringIO objects
for read and write buffers.

methods.name: transport.TZlibTransport.TZlibTransport._init_stats
documentation:
Internal method to reset the internal statistics counters
for compression ratios and bandwidth savings.

methods.name: transport.TZlibTransport.TZlibTransport._init_zlib
documentation:
Internal method for setting up the zlib compression and
decompression objects.

methods.name: transport.TZlibTransport.TZlibTransport.getCompRatio
documentation:
Get the current measured compression ratios (in,out) from
this transport.

Returns a tuple of:
(inbound_compression_ratio, outbound_compression_ratio)

The compression ratios are computed as:
    compressed / uncompressed

E.g., data that compresses by 10x will have a ratio of: 0.10
and data that compresses to half of ts original size will
have a ratio of 0.5

None is returned if no bytes have yet been processed in
a particular direction.

methods.name: transport.TZlibTransport.TZlibTransport.getCompSavings
documentation:
Get the current count of saved bytes due to data
compression.

Returns a tuple of:
(inbound_saved_bytes, outbound_saved_bytes)

Note: if compression is actually expanding your
data (only likely with very tiny thrift objects), then
the values returned will be negative.

methods.name: transport.TZlibTransport.TZlibTransport.isOpen
documentation: Return the underlying transport's open status

methods.name: transport.TZlibTransport.TZlibTransport.open
documentation: Open the underlying transport

methods.name: transport.TZlibTransport.TZlibTransport.listen
documentation: Invoke the underlying transport's listen() method

methods.name: transport.TZlibTransport.TZlibTransport.accept
documentation: Accept connections on the underlying transport

methods.name: transport.TZlibTransport.TZlibTransport.close
documentation: Close the underlying transport,

methods.name: transport.TZlibTransport.TZlibTransport.read
documentation:
Read up to sz bytes from the decompressed bytes buffer, and
read from the underlying transport if the decompression
buffer is empty.

methods.name: transport.TZlibTransport.TZlibTransport.readComp
documentation:
Read compressed data from the underlying transport, then
decompress it and append it to the internal StringIO read buffer

methods.name: transport.TZlibTransport.TZlibTransport.write
documentation:
Write some bytes, putting them into the internal write
buffer for eventual compression.

methods.name: transport.TZlibTransport.TZlibTransport.flush
documentation:
Flush any queued up data in the write buffer and ensure the
compression buffer is flushed out to the underlying transport

methods.name: transport.TZlibTransport.TZlibTransport.cstringio_buf
documentation: Implement the CReadableTransport interface

methods.name: transport.TZlibTransport.TZlibTransport.cstringio_refill
documentation: Implement the CReadableTransport interface for refill

Class that wraps a transport with zlib, compressing writes
and decompresses reads, using the python standard
library zlib module.

classes.name: protocol.TCompactProtocol.TCompactProtocolAccelerated
documentation:
C-Accelerated version of TCompactProtocol.

This class does not override any of TCompactProtocol's methods,
but the generated code recognizes it directly and will call into
our C module to do the encoding, bypassing this object entirely.
We inherit from TCompactProtocol so that the normal TCompactProtocol
encoding can happen if the fastbinary module doesn't work for some
reason.
To disable this behavior, pass fallback=False constructor argument.

In order to take advantage of the C module, just use
TCompactProtocolAccelerated instead of TCompactProtocol.

modules.name: server.TNonblockingServer
documentation:
Implementation of non-blocking server.

The main idea of the server is to receive and send requests
only from the main thread.

The thread pool should be sized for concurrent tasks, not
maximum connections
Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one
or more contributor license agreements. See the NOTICE file
distributed with this work for additional information
regarding copyright ownership. The ASF licenses this file

functions.name: server.TNonblockingServer.locked
documentation: Decorator which locks self.lock.

functions.name: server.TNonblockingServer.socket_exception
documentation: Decorator close object on socket.error.

classes.name: server.TNonblockingServer.Worker

methods.name: server.TNonblockingServer.Worker.run
documentation: Process queries from task queue, stop if processor is None.
Worker is a small helper to process incoming connection.

classes.name: server.TNonblockingServer.Connection

methods.name: server.TNonblockingServer.Connection.read
documentation: Reads data from stream and switch state.

methods.name: server.TNonblockingServer.Connection.write
documentation: Writes data from socket and switch state.

methods.name: server.TNonblockingServer.Connection.ready
documentation:
Callback function for switching state and waking up main thread.

This function is the only function witch can be called asynchronous.

The ready can switch Connection to three states:
    WAIT_LEN if request was oneway.
    SEND_ANSWER if request was processed in normal way.
    CLOSED if request throws unexpected exception.

The one wakes up main thread.

methods.name: server.TNonblockingServer.Connection.is_writeable
documentation: Return True if connection should be added to write list of select

methods.name: server.TNonblockingServer.Connection.is_readable
documentation: Return True if connection should be added to read list of select
it's not necessary, but...

methods.name: server.TNonblockingServer.Connection.is_closed
documentation: Returns True if connection is closed.

methods.name: server.TNonblockingServer.Connection.fileno
documentation: Returns the file descriptor of the associated socket.

methods.name: server.TNonblockingServer.Connection.close
documentation: Closes connection

Basic class is represented connection.

It can be in state:
    WAIT_LEN --- connection is reading request len.
    WAIT_MESSAGE --- connection is reading request.
    WAIT_PROCESS --- connection has just read whole request and
                     waits for call ready routine.
    SEND_ANSWER --- connection is sending answer string (including length
                    of answer).
    CLOSED --- socket was closed and connection should be deleted.

classes.name: server.TNonblockingServer.TNonblockingServer

methods.name: server.TNonblockingServer.TNonblockingServer.setNumThreads
documentation: Set the number of worker threads that should be created.

methods.name: server.TNonblockingServer.TNonblockingServer.prepare
documentation: Prepares server for serve requests.

methods.name: server.TNonblockingServer.TNonblockingServer.wake_up
documentation:
Wake up main thread.

The server usually waits in select call in we should terminate one.
The simplest way is using socketpair.

Select always wait to read from the first socket of socketpair.

In this case, we can just write anything to the second socket from
socketpair.

methods.name: server.TNonblockingServer.TNonblockingServer.stop
documentation:
Stop the server.

This method causes the serve() method to return.  stop() may be invoked
from within your handler, or from another thread.

After stop() is called, serve() will return but the server will still
be listening on the socket.  serve() may then be called again to resume
processing requests.  Alternatively, close() may be called after
serve() returns to close the server socket and shutdown all worker
threads.

methods.name: server.TNonblockingServer.TNonblockingServer._select
documentation: Does select on open connections.

methods.name: server.TNonblockingServer.TNonblockingServer._poll_select
documentation: Does poll on open connections, if available.

methods.name: server.TNonblockingServer.TNonblockingServer.handle
documentation:
Handle requests.

WARNING! You must call prepare() BEFORE calling handle()

methods.name: server.TNonblockingServer.TNonblockingServer.close
documentation: Closes the server.

methods.name: server.TNonblockingServer.TNonblockingServer.serve
documentation:
Serve requests.

Serve requests forever, or until stop() is called.
Non-blocking server.

classes.name: transport.TTransport.CReadableTransport

methods.name: transport.TTransport.CReadableTransport.cstringio_buf
documentation: A cStringIO buffer that contains the current chunk we are reading.
TODO(dreiss): Think about changing this interface to allow us to use
a (Python, not c) StringIO instead, because it allows
you to write after reading.
NOTE: This is a classic class, so properties will NOT work
correctly for setting.

methods.name: transport.TTransport.CReadableTransport.cstringio_refill
documentation:
Refills cstringio_buf.

Returns the currently used buffer (which can but need not be the same as
the old cstringio_buf). partialread is what the C code has read from the
buffer, and should be inserted into the buffer before any more reads.  The
return value must be a new, not borrowed reference.  Something along the
lines of self._buf should be fine.

If reqlen bytes can't be read, throw EOFError.
base class for transports that are readable from C
This class should be thought of as an interface.

classes.name: transport.TTransport.TBufferedTransport

methods.name: transport.TTransport.TBufferedTransport.cstringio_buf
documentation: Implement the CReadableTransport interface.

Class that wraps another transport and buffers its I/O.

The implementation uses a (configurable) fixed-size read buffer
but buffers all writes until a flush is performed.

classes.name: transport.TTransport.TMemoryBuffer

methods.name: transport.TTransport.TMemoryBuffer.__init__
documentation:
value -- a value to read from for stringio

If value is set, this will be a transport for reading,
otherwise, it is for writing

methods.name: transport.TTransport.TMemoryBuffer.cstringio_buf
documentation: Implement the CReadableTransport interface.

Wraps a cBytesIO object as a TTransport.

NOTE: Unlike the C++ version of this class, you cannot write to it
      then immediately read from it.  If you want to read from a
      TMemoryBuffer, you must either pass a string to the constructor.
TODO(dreiss): Make this work like the C++ version.

documentation:
Default SSL version.
For backwards compatibility, it can be modified.
Use __init__ keyword argument "ssl_version" instead.

classes.name: transport.TSSLSocket.TSSLSocket

methods.name: transport.TSSLSocket.TSSLSocket.__init__
documentation:
Positional arguments: `host`, `port`, `unix_socket`

Keyword arguments: `keyfile`, `certfile`, `cert_reqs`,

: `ssl_version`, `ca_certs`,
  `ciphers` (Python 2.7.0 or later),
  `server_hostname` (Python 2.7.9 or later)

Passed to ssl.wrap_socket. See ssl.wrap_socket documentation.

Alternative keyword arguments: (Python 2.7.9 or later)

: `ssl_context`: ssl.SSLContext to be used for SSLContext.wrap_socket
  `server_hostname`: Passed to SSLContext.wrap_socket

Common keyword argument:

: `validate_callback` (cert, hostname) -> None:

  : Called after SSL handshake. Can raise when hostname does not
    match the cert.

  `socket_keepalive` enable TCP keepalive, default off.
New signature
def __init__(self, host='localhost', port=9090, unix_socket=None,
**ssl_args):
Deprecated signature
def __init__(self, host='localhost', port=9090, validate=True,
ca_certs=None, keyfile=None, certfile=None,
unix_socket=None, ciphers=None):

SSL implementation of TSocket

This class creates outbound sockets wrapped using the
python standard ssl module for encrypted connections.
